title: Comprehensions - a loop that makes a list
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A list comprehension builds a list in one line: [expression for item in sequence if condition].
explanation: It's the for loop you write most often, folded into one readable line. [n * n for n in range(4)] is [0, 1, 4, 9]. Add an if to keep only some: [n for n in nums if n > 0]. Reach for it when the whole loop's job is 'make a new list from an old one'.
code_example:
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
evens = [n for n in nums if n % 2 == 0]
print(evens)
hints: The if keeps only the items that pass.
